rules.name: Visual Hierarchy
rules.description: Use size, color, and contrast to guide users to the most important elements first.
rules.application: Make primary actions larger and more prominent than secondary ones.
rules.example: A large CTA button in a hero section with smaller secondary links below.
rules.anti_pattern: All elements the same size and weight, creating a flat, confusing layout.

rules.name: Consistency
rules.description: Maintain uniform patterns for similar elements across the entire design.
rules.application: Use the same button styles, spacing, and typography for similar interactions.
rules.example: All form inputs share the same height, border radius, and label placement.
rules.anti_pattern: Mixing rounded and square buttons randomly throughout the interface.

rules.name: Proximity
rules.description: Group related items together and separate unrelated items with whitespace.
rules.application: Place labels near their associated inputs; group related actions together.
rules.example: Form field labels directly above their inputs with 4px gap.
rules.anti_pattern: Labels far from their inputs, causing users to guess the associations.

rules.name: Alignment
rules.description: Align elements along invisible lines to create order and connection.
rules.application: Use a consistent grid system and align text and elements to it.
rules.example: Left-aligned text blocks with consistent margins creating a clean edge.
rules.anti_pattern: Center-aligning everything, creating a jagged, unstructured appearance.

rules.name: Contrast
rules.description: Use sufficient contrast between foreground and background for readability.
rules.application: Ensure text meets WCAG contrast ratios against its background.
rules.example: Dark text (#1a1a1a) on a light background (#ffffff) for body copy.
rules.anti_pattern: Light gray text on a white background, making content hard to read.

rules.name: White Space
rules.description: Use empty space intentionally to improve readability and focus.
rules.application: Add generous padding and margins around content blocks.
rules.example: A hero section with ample padding letting the headline breathe.
rules.anti_pattern: Cramming content edge-to-edge with no breathing room.

rules.name: Simplicity
rules.description: Remove unnecessary elements; every component should serve a purpose.
rules.application: Eliminate decorative elements that do not support the user's goal.
rules.example: A clean checkout flow with only essential fields and clear progress.
rules.anti_pattern: Adding decorative borders, shadows, and icons to every element.
